data.title: Key facts: Cisco focuses on AI security in Middle East; DinéLink wins award
data.description:
• Cisco Systems is prioritizing AI security in the Middle East, noting that most organizations in the UAE and KSA aim to develop or use AI agents across sectors.1
  • DinéLink, the Community Innovation Challenge winner, uses Cisco tech to improve internet access for the Navajo Nation and includes Cisco Networking Academy training for community empowerment.2

data.title: Key facts: Chevron Exports Crude from Venezuela; Stock Rises with Oil Prices
data.description:
• Chevron is the sole U.S. firm allowed to export crude from Venezuela, with two supertankers recently departing, each carrying about 1.8 million barrels as operations resume.1
  • Chevron's stock climbed in premarket trading as oil prices hit their highest since November, following President Trump's potential 25% tariff on goods from Iran-related countries.2
  • Chevron is set to gain from rising electricity demand fueled by AI, as the growth of these technologies is likely to boost energy resource needs.3

data.title: Key facts: Cardinal Health raises earnings outlook; completes drug deals; launches diabetes program
data.description:
• Cardinal Health raised its fiscal 2026 earnings outlook to at least $10.00 per share, up from $9.65-$9.85, due to strong demand for specialty medicines and solid performance across its segments.123
  • Cardinal Health has completed its transition of distribution agreements for all drugs affected by the 2026 Medicare price negotiations ahead of the year's start.4
  • Cardinal Health launched the ContinuCare Pathway program to simplify diabetes supply management, with Publix Super Markets enrolling its entire network of nearly 1,400 pharmacies in the initiative.56

data.title: Key facts: Boeing delivers 600 aircraft in 2025; secures major orders
data.description:
• Boeing delivered 600 aircraft in 2025, a 72% increase from 2024, with 160 jets in Q4. The company also surpassed Airbus in net orders, securing 1,175 new orders.12
  • Boeing received a major order from Delta Air Lines for up to 60 787-10 Dreamliner jets, including 30 firm orders and 30 options, to boost its long-haul fleet.3
  • Boeing received an order from Aviation Capital Group for 50 737 MAX jets, including 25 737-8 and 25 737-10, with deliveries set from 2026 to 2033.4

data.title: Key facts: Barclays Raises United Airlines Target to $150; Invests $175M in JetZero
data.description:
• Barclays has increased the price target for United Airlines shares from $135.00 to $150.00.1
  • United Airlines invested in JetZero's Series B funding, raising $175 million. This follows a prior investment enabling orders for up to 100 planes, emphasizing its focus on aviation tech.2

data.title: Key facts: Barclays Raises Salesforce Price Target to $338; Slackbot Launched
data.description:
• Barclays has raised its price target for Salesforce shares to $338.00 from a previous target of $330.00.1
  • Salesforce has launched the general availability of Slackbot, a personal agent aimed at improving productivity and streamlining work processes for users.2

data.title: Key facts: Bank of New York Mellon Q4 income rises 26.5%; CEO warns on Fed
data.description:
• Bank of New York Mellon reported Q4 net income of $1.43 billion, or $2.02 per share, a 26.5% increase. Revenue rose 6.8% to $5.18 billion, exceeding estimates.123
  • Bank of New York Mellon CEO Robin Vince warned that undermining the U.S. Federal Reserve's independence could destabilize the bond market and raise interest rates, increasing borrowing costs.45
  • Bank of New York Mellon announced dividends for its preferred stock, payable on March 20, 2026. Amounts vary by series, with record holders as of March 5, 2026.6

data.title: Key facts: B of A Raises IBM Price Target to $335; UBS Highlights Quantum Tech
data.description:
• B of A Securities has increased its price target for IBM shares from $315.00 to $335.00.1
  • UBS analysts emphasized the potential of quantum computing to transform the industry, specifically mentioning IBM's superconducting and trapped-ion systems as key technologies for future advancements.2

data.title: Key facts: Ares Management raises $7.1B for Credit Secondaries; UBS adjusts rating
data.description:
• Ares Management raised about $7.1 billion for its Credit Secondaries strategy, surpassing its $2 billion target with the final closing of the Ares Credit Secondaries Fund.1
  • UBS has maintained a Neutral rating on Ares Management and adjusted its price target for the company's shares from $201.00 to $198.00.2
  • Ares Management Corp. has expanded its partnership with Engie North America, adding a 730 MW portfolio of solar and wind assets in the U.S., with Engie managing the assets.3

data.title: Key facts: Amphenol to Acquire CCS; Citigroup Raises Price Target to $175
data.description:
• Amphenol Corp. (APH) will boost its fiber optic interconnects by acquiring CCS, expected to raise earnings by 4% in 2026 and 16% in the following two years.1
  • Citigroup has increased its price target for Amphenol Corp. (APH) to $175.00 per share, up from the previous target of $160.00.2

data.title: Key facts: Alphabet's Stock Surges 66%; Google Launches AI Shopping Tool
data.description:
• Alphabet's stock hit a 52-week low of $144.70 in April 2025 but surged to finish the year with a 66% gain, driven by a favorable antitrust ruling and strong AI performance.1
  • Google has introduced an AI shopping tool that streamlines product discovery and payment, collaborating with retailers like Wayfair and Chewy, and offers price-drop alerts for users.2
  • TD Cowen has maintained a 'Buy' rating for Alphabet Inc. (Google), reflecting ongoing confidence in the company's performance.3

data.title: Key facts: Adobe shares drop 5.9% after downgrade; Apple launches Creator Studio
data.description:
• Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 5.9% to $308.40 after Oppenheimer downgraded the stock, citing worries about a slowdown in digital media amid the ongoing AI technology shift.12
  • Apple launched its subscription plan for creative software apps, named Apple Creator Studio, priced at $12.99 per month or $129 annually, which is expected to heighten competition for Adobe.3

data.title: Key facts: AbbVie signs Medicaid deal; banker charged with insider trading; UAE launches mental health campaign
data.description:
• AbbVie has signed a three-year deal with the U.S. government to reduce Medicaid drug prices and invest $100 billion in U.S. research and manufacturing over ten years.123
  • Justin Kim, a former Lazard banker, faces insider trading charges linked to AbbVie's $10.1 billion ImmunoGen acquisition, allegedly profiting $41 million from leaked confidential takeover info.4
  • The UAE's Ministry of Health has teamed up with AbbVie to launch the 'Mental Health 360' campaign, aimed at boosting mental health awareness and enhancing community well-being.5

data.title: Wealthfront Fundamentals Seen as OK Despite Short-Term Turbulence — Market Talk
data.description: Wealthfront remains positioned for long-term success despite turbulence in the short-term, JPMorgan analysts write in a note. In the financial services company's first earnings release after its December initial public offering, Wealthfront indicated that some flows were weakening despite a strong equity market, especially cash management segment outflows and investment advisory segment inflows, they write. Still, the analysts highlighted Wealthfront reaching $94 billion in platform assets and the launch of its mortgage product rollout, which will benefit from coinciding with falling interest rates. "We think the long-term drivers of the Wealthfront platform remain intact, but we also acknowledge some near-term choppiness in flows given the evolving macro backdrop," they write. Wealthfront is down 18%. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Yen tumbles on Japan spending fears, dollar broadly higher
data.description:
Key points:
  • Yen tumbles on Japan's fiscal spending concerns
  • Fed has more room to cut rates amid inflation data
  • Traders watch for possible yen intervention by Japan

By Karen Brettell

The Japanese yen tumbled to its weakest level against the U.S. dollar since July 2024 on Tuesday on concerns about looser fiscal and monetary policy in the country.

The dollar was broadly higher following a brief dip on data showing that consumer prices were largely as economists expected last month.

Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi may call an early general election, the head of her party's coalition partner said on Sunday, after media reported she was considering a February vote.

It would give Takaichi a chance to capitalize on the strong public approval ratings she has enjoyed since taking office in October.

“The implications for the yen are quite negative because Takaichi is a dove on both the fiscal and monetary fronts, so fiscally she would be very comfortable with a looser, higher deficit policy,” said Eric Theoret, currency strategist at Scotiabank in Toronto.

The Japanese yen weakened 0.6% against the greenback to 159.11 per dollar.

The rapid weakening in the yen is also putting traders on watch for a possible intervention to shore up the currency.

Japan's Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said she and U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent shared concerns over what she called the yen's recent "one-sided depreciation", as Tokyo stepped up threats of intervention to stem the currency's fall.

The U.S. currency briefly dipped after consumer prices were seen as potentially giving the Federal Reserve more room to cut rates as policymakers balance concerns about still sticky price pressures against a weakening labor market.

"Today's data adds further support to the notion that inflation is trending down," Preston Caldwell, chief U.S. economist at Morningstar, said in a note.

The Consumer Price Index rose 0.3% last month for an annual gain of 2.7%, while core CPI rose by 0.2% in December for a 2.6% year-on-year increase.

Market reaction suggests that traders were positioned for a potentially larger increase in prices, said Theoret, noting risk-sensitive currencies including the Australian dollar rallied after the report.

“There's a concern now that we've maybe reached the local lows in terms of inflation,” Theoret said. “Maybe people were positioned for the alternative, which would have been an upside surprise.”

central bank's 2% target, reiterating this week they need to keep interest rates at current levels until it becomes clearer that price pressures will ease.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro,

was last up 0.28% at 99.15, with the euro down 0.17% at $1.1647.

Sterling weakened 0.23% to $1.3428.

The Australian dollar was last down 0.45% versus the greenback at $0.668, after briefly bouncing to $0.6725 on the data.

The dollar was boosted on Friday after data showed solid jobs growth in December, which further boosted expectations that the U.S. central bank will keep rates on hold at its January 27-28 policy meeting.

Fed funds futures traders' pricing shows that a cut is not seen as likely until June.

Traders are also considering the issue of Fed independence after the U.S. Department of Justice threatened to indict Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell in connection with a building renovation project.

Global central bank officials issued a coordinated statement of support for Powell on Tuesday.

U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to announce in the coming weeks his candidate to replace Powell as chair when his term ends in May. Trump said on Tuesday that December's inflation data supported his push for Powell to cut rates.

Rising geopolitical tensions also remain a focus after the United States took Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro into custody and as protests rock Iran and Trump expresses his desire for the U.S. to acquire Greenland.

Traders are also watching for a Supreme Court decision on the legality of Trump’s tariff policies, which could come as soon as Wednesday.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin gained 3.12% to $93,811.

data.title: These Stocks Are Today's Movers: JPMorgan, Delta, Intel, AMD, Adobe, Moderna, Super Micro, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni and George Glover

Stocks were mostly lower Tuesday after the latest data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics showed inflation held steady in December as prices for goods and services rose 2.7%.

These stocks were making moves:

JPMorgan Chase fell 3.5%. The bank's profit declined 7% in the fourth quarter, even as revenue rose on the back of higher fees and interest income. Earnings of $4.63 a share fell short of analysts' calls for $4.85.

Delta Air Lines declined 2.9%. The legacy carrier beat Wall Street forecasts for adjusted earnings and revenue in the fourth quarter, but issued 2026 guidance that missed expectations. The airline called for 2026 earnings in the range of $6.50 to $7.50 a share, below analysts' calls for $7.28 at the midpoint.

Bank of New York Mellon rose 0.7%. Fourth-quarter adjusted earnings came in at $2.08 a share, topping the $1.99 a share analysts had expected, while total revenue climbed 7% to $5.18 billion, beating calls for $5.14 billion.

L3Harris Technologies gained 0.6%. The defense contractor said it plans to spin off its missile business into a separate public company. The Defense Department will invest $1 billion in the business through a convertible preferred security, which will convert to common equity upon an initial public offering later this year.

Intel climbed 8.4% to $47.74. KeyBanc analyst John Vinh upgraded shares to Overweight from Sector Weight with a $60 price target, pointing to strong demand from cloud service providers.

Advanced Micro Devices received a rating change for the same reason. Shares rose 6.5% to $221.18 after Vinh upgraded the stock to Overweight from Sector Weight and assigned a $270 price target.

Moderna jumped 14%. CEO Stéphane Bancel said the company expects to roll out its combined flu and Covid vaccine over the next two years, with a 2027 launch in Europe following its approval this year and a U.S. launch in 2028.

Super Micro Computer declined 5.8% to $28.38. Goldman Sachs analyst Katherine Murphy assumed coverage of the stock with a Sell rating and price target of $26.

Revvity jumped 3.4% after the health-sciences company raised its full-year outlook ahead of earnings early next month.

Alphabet rose 2.3%. The Google parent's class A shares added 1% on Monday, closing at a record high of $331.86. The gains lifted the search-engine provider's market capitalization above $4 trillion for the first time. Alphabet became the fourth U.S. company to reach this milestone after Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft.

Cardinal Health jumped 3.8%. The healthcare services company said it expects fiscal-year earnings of at least $10 a share, up from a prior range of $9.65 to $9.85. Analysts tracked by FactSet were looking for $9.86 a share. The company also projected over $50 billion in specialty revenue for the fiscal year.

Travere Therapeutics sank 34% after the drugmaker received additional information requests from the Food and Drug Administration to characterize the clinical benefit of Filspari, its drug candidate for a rare kidney disorder.

AbbVie slipped 0.5%. The drug company reached a three-year agreement with the Trump administration to lower the prices of some drugs and invest in U.S. research and development in exchange for tariff exemption.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com and George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Rebounds to Resume Rally
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange rose on Tuesday ahead of Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's visit to China.

Trade and agriculture will be two of the topics up for discussion during Carney's visit. China imposed 100 per cent tariffs of Canadian canola oil and meal, as well as a 75.8 per cent tariff on Canadian canola seed last year.

One analyst doesn't believe there will be any change to the tariffs, but there is a remote possibility for reductions. Another analyst added that Canada's exports won't be enough to sustain canola's rallies on the markets.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture estimated the Canadian canola carryout for 2025-26 at 3.71 million tonnes in its world oilseed report on Monday, 760,000 more than Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada's latest estimate. One of the analysts said he expects the actual number to be more than four million tonnes.

Chicago soyoil and European rapeseed were up, but Malaysian palm oil was down. Crude oil was up nearly US$2 per barrel after drones struck oil tankers at a Russian terminal last night.

At mid-afternoon, the Canadian dollar lost one-tenth of a U.S. cent compared to Monday's close.

There were 70,741 canola contracts traded on Tuesday, compared to Monday when 50,914 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 35,878 of the contracts traded.

Settlement prices are in Canadian dollars per metric tonne.

data.title: Creative Planning Makes Its First International Acquisition. Here's Where. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Kenneth Corbin

Creative Planning, a large registered investment advisory firm, has made its first international acquisition, snapping up Switzerland-based Baseline Wealth Management. The deal closed Jan. 8.

Baseline has offices in Geneva and Zurich, and its staff of 14 manages more than $1 billion in client assets.

The firm caters to high-net-worth individuals around the world, including professional athletes and entrepreneurs. The firm's CEO, Thierry Grin, had a brief career as a professional tennis player.

Creative Planning CEO Peter Mallouk suggests in a statement that this marks the beginning of a European campaign rather than a one-off acquisition.

"We're absolutely thrilled to have Baseline join Creative Planning for our initial expansion into Europe," he says. Baseline serves some U.S. clients, and Mallouk said the firm shares Creative's "core values of integrity, transparency, and a strong fiduciary responsibility to clients."

A Creative Planning spokeswoman declined to comment when asked about Creative Planning's overall strategy for Europe, whether it has an acquisition pipeline, and when the firm might release more news about European acquisitions.

Baseline is registered as a portfolio manager with the Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority. It also maintains registrations with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission and the Canadian Securities Administrators.

Creative took a step toward expanding its international operations in 2020 with the acquisition of Thun Financial Advisors, a then-$600 million firm catering to U.S. clients living overseas. Thun was owned by David Kuenzi, who now serves as Creative's international director of wealth management.

He argues that there is an appetite in Europe for the U.S.-style independent RIA advice model that is grounded in a fiduciary duty and offers access to a broad menu of investment products.

"The European market is currently dominated by proprietary product sales by banks with virtually no planning or fiduciary standard — and it can't be fully served from the United States," Kuenzi says. "Europeans want to work with Europeans, in their language and in their time zone."

Write to advisor.editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Fresh-Focused Grocers Growing Faster Than Value Supermarkets — Market Talk
data.description: Value-focused grocery stores have passed their peak of growth, according to Placer.ai data. Value grocers like Lidl and Aldi that dominated growth through 2024 are still expanding but have been surpassed in growth by fresh-format grocers like Sprouts, the Placer.ai report says. Much of the trade-down movement has already happened and the most affluent shoppers are driving demand for fresh produce and health-focused food. The fact that value and high-end fresh are leading the way for growth reflects a bifurcation in the consumer, Placer.ai notes. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: Minerva secures $440 million for Japan late-stage startup fund, source says
data.description:
By Miho Uranaka

Tokyo-based Minerva Growth Partners has secured around 70 billion yen ($440 million) at the first close for a second fund that aims to invest in late-stage startups, according to a person familiar with the matter.

The firm declined to comment on the amount raised so far but said the fund aims to raise around 200 billion yen in total and that backers include the state-backed Japan Investment Corp (JIC).

The source declined to be identified as the information is not public.

Japan has few high-profile startups, with a shortfall of late-stage capital often blamed for pushing companies to go public while still small. The Tokyo Stock Exchange is moving to tighten its criteria to reduce the amount of small listings.

"The number of companies not rushing to IPO and aiming to go public after growing further is increasing," said Kensuke Murashima, managing partner at Minerva.

Murashima, a former banker at Morgan Stanley, established Minerva in 2020 along with former Mercari CFO Kei Nagasawa and backing from Hong Kong-based Pleiad Investment Advisors.

Minerva's first 19.2 billion yen fund, formed in 2020, included investments in payments company Infcurion , which went public in October.

($1 = 158.7800 yen)

data.title: Front Month ICE Brent Crude Rose 2.51% to Settle at $65.47 — Data Talk
data.description:
Front Month ICE Brent Crude for March delivery gained $1.60 per barrel, or 2.51% to $65.47 today

  • Up for four consecutive sessions
  • Up $5.51 or 9.19% over the last four sessions
  • Largest four day dollar and percentage gain since Wednesday, June 18, 2025
  • Longest winning streak since Tuesday, Dec. 23, 2025, when the market rose for five straight sessions
  • Highest settlement value since Monday, Oct. 27, 2025
  • Off 20.19% from its 52-week high of $82.03 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 11.12% from its 52-week low of $58.92 hit Tuesday, Dec. 16, 2025
  • Down 18.08% from 52 weeks ago
  • Up 9.19% from its 2026 settlement low of $59.96 hit Wednesday, Jan. 7, 2026
  • Off 55.18% from its record high of $146.08 hit Thursday, July 3, 2008
  • Month-to-date it is up 7.59%
  • Year-to-date it is up $4.62 or 7.59%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data
